content.cn_text: 您将听到一场关于维京人生活和文化的讲座。
content.en_text: You will hear a lecture about Viking life and culture.

content.cn_text: 首先，您将有时间查看问题31至40。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 您
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 现在，请仔细听并回答问题。
content.en_text: Now, listen carefully and answer the questions.

content.cn_text: 维京人以其航海技能而闻名，他们不仅是战士，也是商人​​和探险家。
content.en_text: The Vikings, known for their seafaring skills, were not only warriors, but also traders and explorers.

content.cn_text: 他们的航程远远超出了斯堪的纳维亚半岛，远至北美。
content.en_text: Their voyages extended far beyond Scandinavia, reaching as far as North America.

content.cn_text: 证实这一点的一个重要证据是在与原住民聚居地相关的遗址中发现了一枚小小的银币。
content.en_text: One significant piece of evidence confirming this is the discovery of a small silver coin found in a site associated with indigenous settlements.

content.cn_text: 这一发现表明维京商人可能与原住民进行过交流，可能用金属制品或纺织品换取当地资源。
content.en_text: This finding suggests that Viking traders may have engaged in exchanges with indigenous people, possibly bartering metal goods or textiles for local resources.

content.cn_text: 战争在维京社会中扮演着重要角色，他们擅长制造和使用各种武器。
content.en_text: Warfare played an important role in Viking society, and they were skilled in producing and using a variety of weapons.

content.cn_text: 考古发掘出土了一系列军备，包括长矛、斧头，以及一件制作精良的武器，可能兼用于战斗和仪式目的。
content.en_text: Archaeological digs have unearthed a range of armaments, including spears, axes, and an especially well crafted weapon, likely used both in combat and for ceremonial purposes.

content.cn_text: 这些武器通常世代相传，象征着地位和工艺。
content.en_text: These weapons were often passed down through generations, signifying status and craftsmanship.

content.cn_text: 维京人最令人印象深刻的成就之一是其先进的造船技术。他们设计了造型优美、坚固耐用的船只，能够经受长时间的海上航行。
content.en_text: One of the Vikings' most impressive achievements was their advanced shipbuilding techniques. They designed sleek, sturdy vessels capable of enduring long sea voyages.

content.cn_text: 他们的长船的一个主要特征是巨大的船帆，使他们能够利用风力高效地跨越广阔的距离。
content.en_text: A key feature of their longships was a large sail, allowing them to harness wind power and travel efficiently across vast distances.

content.cn_text: 这一创新使他们能够在欧洲及其他地区进行劫掠、贸易和建立定居点。
content.en_text: This innovation enabled them to raid, trade, and establish settlements across Europe and beyond.

content.cn_text: 鉴于海上恶劣的条件，为长途航行保存食物至关重要。维京人采用了多种方法来防止食物变质。
content.en_text: Given the harsh conditions at sea, preserving food for long voyages was crucial. Vikings employed various methods to prevent spoilage.

content.cn_text: 一种常用的技术是风干或腌制肉类，但他们也大量依赖一种天然防腐剂，主要是盐。
content.en_text: One commonly used technique involved drying or curing meat, but they also relied heavily on a natural preservative, mainly salt.

content.cn_text: 这确保了他们的补给在长时间内保持可食用，尤其是在他们远航到遥远土地时。
content.en_text: This ensured that their supplies remained edible for extended periods, particularly during their voyages to distant lands.

content.cn_text: 维京人的日常生活与现代截然不同。例如，他们的饮用器皿通常由天然材料制成，而不是玻璃或陶瓷。
content.en_text: Every day life among the Vikings was quite different from modern times. For instance, their drinking vessels were often made from natural materials rather than glass or ceramics.

content.cn_text: 一个常见的选择是角，它既轻便又耐用。另一方面，他们的餐具比我们今天使用的要简单。
content.en_text: A common choice was a horn, which was both lightweight and durable. Eating utensils on the other hand were simpler than those we used today.

content.cn_text: 维京人并非只使用勺子和刀子，他们通常携带一个叉子，用于多种目的，包括辅助进食，甚至作为小工具。
content.en_text: Rather than using spoons and knives exclusively, Vikings commonly carried a fork, which served multiple purposes, including assisting in eating and even as a small tool.

content.cn_text: 维京水手经常遇到风暴和汹涌的水域，这常常导致船只受损。然而，他们非常擅长维护和修复他们的船只。
content.en_text: Viking sailors frequently encountered storms and rough waters, which often led to ship damage. However, they were highly skilled in maintaining and restoring their vessels.
Rather than abandoning damaged ships, they made on-the-spot repairs reinforcing wooden planks, sealing leaks, and replacing worn-out parts.

content.cn_text: 他们修理船只的能力确保了他们在漫长而危险的海上旅程中生存下来。维京人也具有从原材料中制作物品的卓越能力。
content.en_text: Their ability to repair ships ensured their survival during long and dangerous sea journeys. Vikings also had a remarkable ability to craft objects from raw materials.

content.cn_text: 他们特别擅长使用桨，将其制成工具和武器。他们所依赖的最重要的材料之一是金属，他们将其塑造成剑、盔甲和农具。
content.en_text: They were particularly adept at working with oars, transforming them into tools and weapons. One of the most essential materials they relied on was metal, which they shaped into swords, armor, and farming implements.

content.cn_text: 像许多古代社会一样，维京人通过口头传统而非书面记录来传承他们的历史。
content.en_text: Like many ancient societies, Vikings passed down their history through oral traditions rather than written records.

content.cn_text: 他们经常围坐在火堆旁或大厅里，聆听英雄故事和神话的朗诵。这些叙事通过诗歌得以保存和传承，使他们的文化遗产世代相传。
content.en_text: They often gathered around fires, or in great halls, to listen to recitations of heroic tales and myths. These narratives were preserved and transmitted through poems, which kept their cultural heritage alive for generations.

content.cn_text: 与当时一些同时代社会不同，维京女性拥有某些合法权利。她们能够继承财产和管理生意。
content.en_text: Unlike in some contemporary societies at the time, Viking women had certain legal rights. They were able to inherit property and manage businesses.

content.cn_text: 更值得注意的是，如果她们希望结束婚姻，她们有权诉诸法庭。在这种情况下，女性可以正式请求离婚，这在许多其他中世纪文化中是相对不常见的做法。
content.en_text: More notably, they had the right to approach a court, if they wished to end their marriage. In such cases, a woman could formally request a divorce, a practice that was relatively uncommon in many other medieval cultures.

content.cn_text: 欲了解更多信息，请访问我们的网站 WWW.TV.TV.TV
content.en_text: For more information, visit our website at WWW.TV.TV.TV

content.cn_text: 欲了解更多信息，请访问我们的网站 WWW.TV.TV.TV.TV
content.en_text: For more information, visit our website at WWW.TV.TV.TV.TV

groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: A 31. _____ was discovered, proving that there was interaction between the Vikings and Indigenous people in North America.
They relied on a variety of 32. ______ to defend themselves and for hunting.
They were exceptional shipbuilders and constructed vessels with a 33. _____ that enabled long-distance travel.
To ensure food lasted during voyages, they used 34. _____ for preservation.
For drinking, Vikings often used a 35. _____ and their common eating utensil was a 36 _____ .
For drinking, Vikings often used a 35. _____ and their common eating utensil was a 36 _____ .
In order to sustain their sea journeys, they had the ability to 37. _____ damaged ships.
Much of their history and stories were passed down through 39. _____ .
Viking society granted women certain rights, including the ability to go to court to request a 40. _____ .

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each anwer.
The Vikings
Trade and Exploration
• Vikings were skilled traders and explorers, travelling great distances by sea.
• A $31$ was discovered, proving that there was interaction between the Vikings and Indigenous people in North America.
• They relied on a variety of $32$ to defend themselves and for hunting.
Ships and Daily Life
• They were exceptional shipbuilders and constructed vessels with a $33$ that enabled long-distance travel.
• To ensure food lasted during voyages, they used $34$ for preservation.
• For drinking, Vikings often used a $35$ and their common eating utensil was a $36$ .
Skills and Arts
• In order to sustain their sea journeys, they had the ability to $37$ damaged ships.
• They crafted essential tools using $38$ .
• Much of their history and stories were passed down through $39$ .
Society and Law
• Viking society granted women certain rights, including the ability to go to court to request a $40$ .
